steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what motivates Tia Ana's repeated checking. Look for the detail in the passage that directly explains why she keeps coming in to see if guests have left.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'she needs assistance elsewhere, but Salomé is unavailable': the passage never mentions Tia Ana needing help from Salomé or being needed elsewhere in the house. ✗
  • Choice B — 'she is anxious for the gathering to disperse so she can ready the space for her own needs': the passage states the parlor is her special province for her school, and that after every salon it is “never in order for its transformation back to a classroom the following morning.” Her repeated checking is explained directly by this. ✓
  • Choice C — 'she wants to share plans for a new school with the guests': the passage says nothing about Tia Ana wanting to talk to the guests—she wants them gone, not engaged. ✗
  • Choice D — 'she finds the guests dull': her motivation is about reclaiming her space, not her opinion of the guests' personalities. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
